description: PRD Doctor Fix - first evolving PRD slice

finalSuccessCriteria.description: Ralph can repair an invalid prd.json in one doctor pass by detecting the current validation issue, injecting that issue into a DOCTOR.md prompt, running the selected AI tool once, and revalidating the file afterward.
acceptanceCriteria: A DOCTOR.md prompt exists at the project root and in CLI templates alongside DEVELOPER.md and PLANNER.md
The CLI exposes a fix/doctor flow that detects current prd.json validation errors before invoking the AI tool
The doctor prompt sent to the AI tool includes the exact detected validation issue(s) in a clearly labeled section
The fix flow runs a single doctor pass, then revalidates prd.json and reports success only when validation passes
If prd.json is already valid, the fix flow exits cleanly without invoking a doctor pass
ralph init installs DOCTOR.md for new projects
A manual CLI verification demonstrates repairing a deliberately broken prd.json in one doctor pass

planning.currentObjective: Add the single-pass PRD doctor prompt and CLI fix path that injects current validation errors

prdChain.objective: Implement a one-pass PRD doctor fix flow

userStories.title: Add single-pass PRD doctor fix flow
userStories.description: As a Ralph user, I want Ralph to detect prd.json validation issues and run a DOCTOR.md-powered fix pass with the current issue injected, so broken PRD metadata can be repaired without entering the develop/plan loop.
acceptanceCriteria: Add DOCTOR.md at the repository root and cli/templates/DOCTOR.md with instructions to repair only prd.json/progress metadata, preserve user intent, avoid product code changes, and complete in one pass
Update ralph init so DOCTOR.md is copied with DEVELOPER.md and PLANNER.md
Add a CLI fix/doctor command or equivalent fix flow that accepts --tool and --dir options consistent with ralph run
Before invoking the AI tool, call the existing prd.json validator and inject the exact current validation errors into the DOCTOR.md prompt under a clear heading
If prd.json is already valid, print a clear no-op message and do not invoke the AI tool
If prd.json is invalid, invoke the selected AI tool exactly once with the doctor prompt, then re-run validation and exit nonzero while printing remaining errors if validation still fails
Update README or CLI help documentation for the new fix flow
Typecheck passes (`npm run typecheck` exits 0 in cli/)
Manual CLI verification repairs a deliberately broken temporary prd.json in one doctor pass
